title: Multi-batch process for generating precipitated calcium carbonate
abstract: Multi-batch processes for forming precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC) using one or more reactors are described. A CO2 source and a calcium source are reacted in water partially or completely, and with or without additives to form calcium carbonate, which may be separated from the water as a precipitated calcium carbonate product having single or multiple modes, structures, and/or morphologies. In other embodiments, more than one reactor may be used to form PCC products having different properties, which are products are then combined. One or more reactors may have an output stream of PCC or excess reagents diverted to feed into one or more other reactors. The diverted output stream may be concentrated or modified by other means.
claims: Claims CLAIMS 1. A multi-batch process for generating precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC), comprising: reacting a CO 2 source, a calcium source, and water in a first reactor to produce a mixture of calcium carbonate and water; and filtering the mixture to produce PCC and a water phase. 2. The multi-batch process of claim 1, wherein the calcium source is converted to calcium chloride prior to reaction with CO. 2 3. The multi-batch process of claim 2, wherein the calcium source is converted to calcium chloride in the first reactor prior to addition of the CO 2 source. 4. The multi-batch process of claim 2, wherein the calcium source is converted to calcium chloride in the first reactor in the presence of the CO 2 source. 5. The multi-batch process of claim 1 , further comprising treating the mixture with a fatty acid, a fatty acid derivative salt and/or a dispersant, wherein the treating is before, during and/or after the reacting. 6. The multi-batch process of claim 1, further comprising feeding the water phase to the first reactor. 7. The multi-batch process of claim 6, wherein the water phase comprises an acid or an acidic compound. 8. The multi-batch process of claim 1, further comprising adding a dopant and/or a seed before, during or after the reacting. 9. The multi-batch process of claim 1, wherein the PCC is at least one selected from the group consisting of single-modal, single-polymorph, and single-structure. 10, The multi-batch process of claim 1 , wherein the PCC is at least one selected from the group consisting of multi-modal, multi-polymorph, and multi-structure. 11, The multi-batch process of claim 1, wherein the process is performed in from 2 to IS reactors. 12, The multi-batch process of claim 11, wherein the 2 to 15 reactors may be operated in series or in parallel, wherein each reactor may have the same or different operating conditions, and the same or different starting materials, relative to other reactors in the process. 13;: A two- reactor process for generating precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC), comprising: feeding a first C0 2 source, a first calcium source, and water to a first reactor to produce an unreacted calcium source and a first mixture of a first calcium carbonate and water; feeding at least a portion of the first mixture, the unreacted calcium source and/or a second calcium source different or the same as the first calcium source, and a second CO 2 source to a second reactor to produce a second calcium carbonate in mixture with the first calcium carbonate and the water, forming a second mixture; and filtering the second mixture to produce PCC and a water phase, wherein the PCC is multi-modal, multi-polymorph, and/or multi-structure. 14. The two-reactor process of claim 13, further comprising adding a dopant and/or a seed to the first reactor and/or to the second reactor. 15. The two- reactor process of claim 13, further comprising treating the first mixture and/or the second mixture with a fatty acid, a fatty acid derivative salt, and/or a dispersant, wherein the treating is before, during and/or after the feeding step for the specified mixture. 16;: A serial triple-reactor process for generating precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC), comprising: feeding a first CO 2 source, a first calcium source, and water to a first reactor to produce a first unreacted calcium source and a first mixture of a first calcium carbonate and water; feeding at least a portion of the first mixture, the first unreacted calcium source and/or a second calcium source which may be the same or different from the first calcium source, and a second CO 2 source to a second reactor to produce a second unreacted calcium source and second mixture of a second calcium carbonate in mixture with the first calcium carbonate and water; feeding at least a portion of the second mixture, the second unreacted calcium source and/or a third calcium source which may be the same or different from either or both of the first and second calcium sources, and a third CO. source to a third reactor to produce a third mixture of a third calcium carbonate in mixture with the first and second calcium carbonate and water; and filtering the third mixture to produce PCC and a water phase, wherein the PCC is multi-modal, multi-polymorph, and/or multi-structure. 17. The serial triple-reactor process of claim 16, further comprising filtering a fully reacted mixture of the first calcium carbonate and water from the first reactor to produce a second PCC and a second water phase, wherein the second PCC is multimodal, multi-polymorph, and/or multi-structure. 18. The serial triple-reactor process of claim 16, further comprising treating the the first, second, and/or third mixture with a fatty acid, a fatty acid derivative salt, and/or a dispersant, wherein the treating is performed before, during and/or after feeding the specified mixture. 19. The serial triple-reactor process of claim 16, further comprising adding a dopant and/or seed to the first, second, and/or third reactor. 20, The serial triple-reactor process of claim 16, further comprising feeding a product from the third reactor to the second reactor. 21. The serial triple-reactor process of claim 16, further comprising treating the first, second, and/or third mixture with a fatty acid, a fatty acid derivative salt, and/or a dispersant. 22. A triple-reactor process for generating precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC), comprising: feeding a first CO 2 source, a first calcium source, and a first water source to a first reactor to produce a first mixture of a first calcium carbonate and water; filtering at least a portion of the first mixture to produce a first PCC and a first water phase; feeding a second CO 2 source, a second calcium source, and a second water source to a second reactor to produce a second mixture of a second calcium carbonate and water; filtering at least a portion of the second mixture to produce a second PCC and a second water phase feeding a third CO 2 source, a third calcium source, and a third water source to a third reactor to produce a third mixture of a third calcium carbonate and water; and filtering at least a portion of the third mixture to produce a third PCC and a third water phase, wherein the first PCC, the second PCC, and/or the third PCC is multi-modal, multi- polymorph, and/or multi-structure. 23:, The triple-reactor process of claim 22, wherein a fatty acid, fatty acid derivative salt, and/or a dispersant is added to the first mixture, the second mixture, and/or the third mixture; and/or a dopant and/or a seed is added to the first, second, and/or third reactor. 24. The triple-reactor process of claim 22, further comprising feeding the second reactor with a product from the first reactor. 25. The triple-reactor process of claim 24, wherein a fatty acid, fatty acid derivative salt, and/or dispersant is added to the first mixture, the second mixture, and/or the third mixture; and/or a dopant and/or a seed is added to the first, second and/or third reactor.
